title: Calcitrapoides micracantha

Calcitrapa, also known as Calcitrapa micracantha, is a plant species in the family Brassicaceae. Calcitrapa is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calcitrapa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calcitrapa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
